mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy economy, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving S&P 500 forward returns. This reflects a fundamental improvement in economic activity that supports equity valuations.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth, but S&P 500 20-day returns are predominantly negative over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above a certain threshold, it signals potential monetary tightening by the Fed, which can negatively impact equity valuations by increasing discount rates and raising the cost of capital for businesses.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL exceeds the specified threshold, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 70% of such triggered instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand. This synergistic strength signals a robust economic environment that supports higher corporate revenues and thus higher equity prices.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST growth conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of occurrences over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate (UNRATE) in an expansionary regime suggests the economy is nearing full employment. This can lead to wage inflation and eventually tighter monetary policy, negatively impacting equities by compressing profit margins and increasing discount rates.
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 6.0% during an expansionary period, but S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive (e.g., > 60% positive) in such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a strong economic environment, which typically translates into higher corporate profits and thus supports positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate improved earnings.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth consistently exceeds 2.5% for two consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat over the subsequent 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals a weakening manufacturing sector and broader economic slowdown. This typically leads to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns as economic activity contracts.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A very low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment. This environment supports higher equity valuations by making borrowing cheaper and boosting corporate activity.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for an extended period (e.g., 6 months), but S&P 500 5-day returns are consistently negative (e.g., average return < 0) during that time.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment (UNRATE) and strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market. This boosts consumer confidence and spending, translating to positive equity market performance due to increased demand and corporate revenues.
failure_condition: If both UNRATE and PAYEMS conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of occurrences over any 5-year rolling window.
